content: Added legal analysis as third analytical pillar alongside tape analytics and portfolio analytics

metadata.reason: Document review of Klaim MMA/MRPA revealed critical parameter mismatches

content: Tape vs Portfolio metric duality (general principle, candidate for ANALYSIS_FRAMEWORK.md codification). Metrics grounded in face value over a long history (WAL, vintage rates, lifetime PAR, concentration HHI) drift over time as old positions accumulate. For Tape Analytics — the lens for learning about a company / product — metrics should report a stale-filtered version that strips operationally-resolved-but-still-flagged deals. For Portfolio Analytics — the lens for direct facility risk under covenant — metrics report the unfiltered covenant-binding version. This duality should be a default for any metric where the tape and the facility-eligible pool are different sets. Klaim session 30 surfaced this with WAL Total (137d unfiltered vs 79d operational), but the principle generalises beyond Klaim and beyond WAL. Suggested elevation point: Section 6 (Denominator Discipline) or new Section 17 (Tape vs Portfolio reporting doctrine). Triggered by: 16.5% zombie cohort on Klaim Apr 15 silently dragging WAL Total upward. Prereqs before codification: at least one other asset class case where the same pattern is documented (suggest checking Aajil and Tamara for status-hygiene tails in their next reviews).

metadata.source: session 30 Klaim WAL Total redesign discussion
